raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Student Civic and Voter Empowerment Act aims to educate, engage, and empower youth voters.",
    "During the 2014 election, only 8.2% of eligible 18-24 year olds voted.",
    "In the 2018 midterm election, 27.5% of 18-24 year olds voted, showing an increase from 2014.",
    "AB 963 designates a Civic and Voter Empowerment Coordinator on each campus to develop a Civic and Voter Empowerment Action Plan.",
    "Civic and Voter Empowerment Coordinators will facilitate events focused on civic engagement and voter turnout.",
    "Research indicates that when campuses allocate resources for civic and voter empowerment, student voter turnout increases.",
    "AB 963 may decrease barriers that students face when wanting to become civically engaged.",
    "California has 147 public universities and colleges with an estimated 3.1 million students.",
    "Providing a voting coordinator on each campus will empower students to participate in the political process.",
    "The Student Civic and Voter Empowerment Program is expected to lead to greater collaboration between the Secretary of State’s office and California’s colleges and universities.",
    "The act aims to develop lifelong voting habits among students."
  ]
}
```
